title: Velvety Salty Japanese Pork Curry
description: A creamy, caramelized pork curry packed with allspice and low-sodium flavors, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add sliced onion and stir until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and allspice, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add pork cubes and brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in water and bring to a simmer; cover and reduce heat to low for 20 minutes until pork is tender.
Stir in Japanese curry roux, breaking it apart; let it melt completely and thicken, about 5 minutes.
Add reduced-sodium soy sauce and mirin, stirring to combine and creating a rich, velvety sauce.
Simmer uncovered for another 5 minutes, letting flavors meld.
Serve hot over steamed rice, garnished with toasted sesame seeds if desired.
